# fix(scheduler): /redteam round-2 — observability + ctx-manager (#910)

<!-- Generated by SessionEnd hook from a commit matching a journal-worthy pattern.
     Next session: review, then promote to a real journal entry OR delete. -->

**Commit**: `dc182dcc3266` — fix(scheduler): /redteam round-2 — observability + ctx-manager (#910)

**Body**:

Cross-agent /redteam round 2: security-reviewer CONVERGED-CLEAN; reviewer
+ analyst NEEDS-FIX with 2 distinct MED findings.

- Reviewer MED-1 (LocalRuntime.execute deprecation): wrap runtime in
  context-manager form to silence the DeprecationWarning the retry loop
  multiplied by `max_attempts` per fire. `contextlib.nullcontext` fallback
  preserves backward compat for runtime_factory returns / deterministic
  test stand-ins that satisfy the runtime protocol but not the
  context-manager protocol (per `framework-first.md` § "Drive The Data,
  Not The Dispatch").
- Analyst M1 (successful-retry observability gap, observability.md Rule 7):
  emit symmetric WARN "Scheduled execution recovered after retries" when
  attempt > 1 succeeds. Operators dashboard retry-recoveries separately
  from first-attempt successes; mirrors the existing exhausted-retries
  WARN at bubble-up.
- Analyst L2 (docstring): document `dispatch_via=` mutual-exclusion in
  schedule_cron's `retry:` parameter description + Raises clause.
- New test asserts the recovered-after-retries WARN log fires.
- Regression unblocked: tests/unit/scheduler/test_dispatch.py
  ::test_in_process_fallback_executes_workflow uses _RecordingRuntime
  (deterministic adapter without __enter__/__exit__) — nullcontext
  fallback handles it.

Test results: 17 passed (16 retry + 1 unblocked dispatch test). Full
scheduler regression: 68 passed, 1 pre-existing failure (#871, out of
scope, documented), 2 pre-existing skips.

Trade-off: replaces 1× DeprecationWarning per attempt (which would become
an error in v0.12.0) with 1× RuntimeWarning per fire from
LocalRuntime.__exit__ pool-cleanup path (local.py:1453,
`coroutine 'AsyncSQLDatabaseNode.clear_shared_pools' was never awaited`).
Pre-existing in LocalRuntime.__exit__ — surfaced because scheduler is now
the first caller using context-manager form. Same warning count, less
severe class. Filing follow-up issue against LocalRuntime cleanup at
PR-merge time per `zero-tolerance.md` Rule 1b.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
